settings.autoSaveFailedDetailBody: Your changes are still on this screen and have not been written locally yet. Fix the issue, then try again. Reason: {{error}}
settings.validationNoticeBody: Check the General tab for details.
settings.closeSaveBlocked: Could not close yet because some settings still need attention
settings.remoteApiKeyTitle: This looks like a remote service
settings.remoteApiKeyBody: The {{service}} base URL is not local and the API key is currently blank. If your provider requires authentication, add the key before testing.
settings.connectionErrorUnauthorized: {{service}} rejected this test. Check the API key, model name, and account permissions first.
settings.connectionErrorForbidden: {{service}} received the request, but the current account or token is not allowed to use this endpoint or model.
settings.connectionErrorNotFound: That {{service}} path or model could not be found. Check the base URL and make sure the provider supports this API route.
settings.connectionErrorTimeout: {{service}} did not respond before the timeout. Check the network, current service load, or try increasing the timeout and test again.
settings.connectionErrorNetwork: YAT could not reach {{service}}. Make sure the base URL is reachable, the service is running, and nothing is blocking it via firewall or proxy settings.

overview.title: Finish the basic setup
overview.desc: Set speech, output, and required permissions first.

summary.speechPending: Finish the STT endpoint and model setup first.
summary.polishPending: Polishing is enabled, but the LLM setup is still incomplete.
summary.outputAutoPaste: Results will paste into the current cursor position.
summary.outputClipboard: Results will only be copied to the clipboard.

setup.title: Recommended first checks
setup.desc: Start with speech, output, and wording preferences.
setup.speechPending: The STT endpoint and model are not fully set up yet.
setup.outputAutoPaste: The result will be pasted into the current cursor position.
setup.outputClipboard: The result will only be copied to the clipboard.
setup.promptReady: You already have {{count}} vocabulary entries or custom instructions.
setup.promptPending: You are still using the default instructions with an empty vocabulary list.
setup.promptOptional: Polishing is off. You can set this later.

permissions.title: Platform permissions
permissions.desc: Microphone access, hotkeys, and auto-paste differ by platform. Check limits and permissions first to reduce troubleshooting time.
permissions.pactlBody: pactl (PulseAudio/PipeWire tool) is not installed. Background audio control during recording will not work. Please install pulseaudio-utils or pipewire-pulse.
permissions.playerctlBody: playerctl (MPRIS media control tool) is not installed. Pause-media during recording will not work. Please install playerctl.
permissions.macosMicrophoneBody: If microphone access was denied earlier, reopen it under System Settings → Privacy & Security → Microphone and allow YAT again.
permissions.macosMicrophoneDenied: Microphone access has been denied. Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security → Microphone to allow YAT.
permissions.macosAccessibilityBody: If you want auto-paste to work, YAT usually also needs to be allowed under System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility.
permissions.macosScreenRecordingBody: Some features may require screen recording permission. You can allow YAT under System Settings → Privacy & Security → Screen Recording.
permissions.windowsMicrophoneBody: Open Settings → Privacy & security → Microphone and confirm desktop apps are allowed to use the mic.
permissions.windowsMicrophoneDenied: Microphone access has been denied. Go to Settings → Privacy & security → Microphone to allow desktop apps.
permissions.windowsAutoPasteBody: If the target app runs elevated and YAT does not, simulated paste can fail. Clipboard-only mode is usually the safer fallback.
permissions.linuxMicrophoneBody: Make sure your desktop environment, packaging format, or portal setup allows microphone access.
permissions.linuxHotkeyBody: YAT currently relies on an X11-style global hotkey listener. In native Wayland sessions, hotkeys may fail completely unless your desktop environment provides compatible support.
permissions.unknownBody: If recording, hotkeys, or paste feels unreliable, check microphone, keyboard monitoring, and accessibility-related permissions first.
permissions.waylandBody: YAT currently depends on X11-style global hotkeys, so support under native Wayland may be limited. Try XWayland, or check whether your desktop environment offers global shortcut support.
permissions.openSettingsFailed: Could not open system settings directly. Please follow the path shown in the card and open it manually.

recent.title: Latest result
recent.desc: The latest transcription or polishing result.
recent.emptyDesc: Record a short sample and the newest result will appear here.

pasteFailSuggest.title: Auto-paste keeps failing
pasteFailSuggest.body: Recent auto-paste attempts all fell back to the clipboard. If you want something steadier, switch to clipboard-only mode.

general.recordingHotkeyBody: Hold one key for hold-to-talk, press twice for double-tap, press multiple keys together for a combo, or tap once for single-tap.
general.recordingWaitingKeys: Waiting for key input (press Esc to cancel)
general.hotkeyValidationMissingKey: Enter a key name first. Escape is reserved for cancel, so choose another key.
general.hotkeyValidationEscapeReserved: Escape is reserved for cancelling recordings and cannot be used as the hotkey or modifier.
general.hotkeyValidationUnsupportedKey: Use a supported key name: letters, digits, F1–F12, arrow keys, punctuation keys, modifier keys, navigation keys (Home / End / Insert / Delete / Page Up / Page Down, etc.) or numpad keys.
general.hotkeyValidationMissingModifier: Combo mode needs a main key plus at least one helper key.
general.hotkeyValidationUnsupportedModifier: Use a supported key name for helper keys, such as Ctrl, Alt, Shift, Cmd, or another supported single key.
general.hotkeyValidationSameKey: Do not repeat the same key inside one combo.
general.hotkeyValidationDoubleTapRange: Double-tap interval must stay between 100ms and 1000ms.
general.autoStartHint: Launch YAT in the background after sign-in.
general.startMinimizedHint: Launch without opening the main window. Reopen YAT from the {{place}}.
general.closeToTray: Keep running when the window closes
general.closeToTrayHint: Closing the window hides YAT instead of quitting. Reopen it from the {{place}}.
general.maxRecordingHint: Limit the length of a single recording.
general.autoPasteDesc: Paste into the current input right away.
general.alwaysCopyToggleHint: Keep a copy in the clipboard even when paste succeeds.
general.timeoutHint: Cancel the request if it still has not finished after this long.
general.maxRetriesHint: How many times to retry automatically after failure.
general.microphoneHint: If you just plugged in a device, refresh first.
general.soundEffectsHint: Play sound cues on start, stop, or failure.
general.backgroundAudioHint: Leave other audio alone, lower it, or mute it while recording.
general.backgroundAudioOffDesc: Do not change other audio while recording.
general.backgroundAudioDuckDesc: Temporarily lower other audio and restore it when recording ends.
general.backgroundAudioDuckAmountBody: Other audio will be reduced by {{percent}}%, leaving about {{remaining}}% while you record.
general.autoPauseMediaHint: Pause media when recording starts, then resume when finished.
general.autoPauseMediaHintMacos: Pause media when recording starts, then resume when finished. Supports Music, Spotify, and the active media source.
general.autoPauseMediaHintLinux: Pause media when recording starts, then resume when finished. Uses MPRIS (requires playerctl).
general.autoPauseMediaDetailMacos: Supports Music, Spotify, and the active media source.
general.backgroundAudioPactlMissingBody: Background audio control requires pactl. Please install pulseaudio-utils or pipewire-pulse and restart YAT.
general.autoPausePlayerctlMissingBody: Pause-media requires playerctl. Please install it and restart YAT.
general.autoPauseAccessibilityRequiredBody: Pause-media on macOS requires Accessibility permission. Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility to allow YAT.
general.autoPasteAccessibilityRequiredBody: Auto-paste on macOS requires Accessibility permission. Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility to allow YAT.
general.hotkeyDesc: Set a hotkey that is unlikely to misfire.
general.recordingDesc: Adjust the recording device, duration, sound cues, and background audio.
general.outputDesc: Choose whether the result pastes immediately or only goes to the clipboard.
general.appearanceDesc: Set the default theme and interface language.
general.closeToTrayNotice: YAT is still running in the {{place}}. Reopen it from the icon.
general.windowBehaviorDesc: Control launch, closing, and background behavior.

stt.baseUrlHint: Use the root URL of an OpenAI-compatible API. YAT will append /audio/transcriptions automatically.
stt.apiKeyHint: Enter an endpoint URL above first.
stt.apiKeyHintRemote: Remote providers usually require an API key.
stt.apiKeyHintLocal: Optional for localhost or private-network STT endpoints that do not require auth.
stt.modelHint: Enter the model name from your provider, e.g. whisper-large-v3-turbo
stt.quickStartDesc: Set the endpoint, model, and API key if needed, then run a connection test.
stt.connectionDesc: Make sure the base URL and model are correct. API keys are optional for local no-auth endpoints.
stt.validationDesc: Send a short silent sample to confirm the STT request works end to end.
stt.validationSuccessBody: Your current STT settings responded successfully.

llm.enabledHint: When off, YAT outputs the raw STT result directly for speed.
llm.baseUrlHint: Enter the root URL for your OpenAI-compatible endpoint. YAT appends /chat/completions automatically.
llm.apiKeyHint: Enter an endpoint URL above first.
llm.apiKeyHintRemote: Remote providers usually require an API key.
llm.apiKeyHintLocal: Optional for localhost or private-network LLM endpoints that do not require auth.
llm.modelHint: Enter the model name from your provider, e.g. gpt-4o-mini
llm.modeDesc: Turn it on when you want cleaner or more consistent text.
llm.connectionDesc: YAT uses /chat/completions. Make sure your provider and model support that path. API keys are optional for local no-auth endpoints.
llm.visionNoticeBody: When Screen capture is on, YAT sends one screenshot with your dictated text to the configured provider. If the model does not support images, YAT automatically falls back to text-only.
llm.validationDesc: Send a minimal request to confirm the endpoint and API key work.
llm.validationSuccessBody: Your current polishing settings responded successfully.
llm.disabledNoticeBody: Recording and output still work normally. Come back later if you need it.

prompt.pageDesc: Set text style and reference context. Vocabulary is sent along as preferred terminology.
prompt.userInstructionsDesc: Describe the output style, structure, or tone you want. Use vocabulary for names, terms, and preferred spelling.
prompt.userInstructionsPlaceholder: Keep the casual tone, break long thoughts into short paragraphs, and keep product names in English…
prompt.userInstructionsHint: Describe the result you want. You do not need to write search-and-replace rules.
prompt.contextSourcesTooltip: Extra reference info means text or screen content captured when recording starts and sent together with your dictated text for polishing. More enabled items means more data is sent.
prompt.contextSourcesDesc: Enable these only when the model needs more context about what you are continuing.
prompt.contextEnabledBody: {{count}} extra item(s) will be sent when recording starts. Keep only what truly helps to reduce privacy, speed, and cost impact.
prompt.contextClipboardHint: Read clipboard text when recording starts so the model can continue from what you just copied.
prompt.contextSelectionHintShort: Read the current selection when recording starts so you can revise or continue it.
prompt.contextSelectionHint: In some apps, reading the selection may briefly affect your clipboard.
prompt.showAdvancedContextHint: Expand this area to choose more context sources to send along.
prompt.contextActiveAppHint: Record the active app so the model can better infer the current task.
prompt.contextActiveAppMacNote: macOS requires Screen Recording permission to read window titles.
prompt.contextInputFieldHint: Read the full text of the focused input field so the model can continue from what is already there.
prompt.contextInputFieldNoteWindows: Only supported in some apps; browsers typically do not work.
prompt.contextInputFieldNoteMacos: Requires Accessibility permission; only supported in some apps.
prompt.contextScreenshotHint: Capture the screen when recording starts and send it as extra visual context.
prompt.contextScreenshotNote: Turn this on only when the screen does not contain sensitive information. It increases upload size and API usage. If the model does not support images, YAT falls back to text-only.
prompt.systemPromptDesc: This is the lowest-level rule source. Change it only when you truly need to override base behavior.
prompt.llmDisabledBody: Prompt instructions and vocabulary are only sent when the LLM pass is enabled. If you just want fast raw output for now, leaving polishing off is fine.
prompt.previewDesc: Shows how the system prompt, custom instructions, and vocabulary are combined before sending.
prompt.advancedWarningTitle: Edit this only when you need to change the base rules
prompt.advancedWarningBody: The system prompt affects every output. Over-editing it usually reduces stability, so try simple instructions first.

presets.keepToneValue: Keep the original tone. Do not over-formalize the writing.
presets.bulletsValue: If the content contains multiple items, turn them into a bullet list.
presets.formalValue: Make the text cleaner and clearer without changing the meaning.
presets.meetingValue: If the content sounds like tasks or updates, format it like concise meeting notes.

vocabulary.pageDesc: Add product names, proper nouns, project jargon, or preferred phrases so the model can use them more consistently.
vocabulary.inputHint: Only enter the final spelling or phrasing you want to see. One entry per line.
vocabulary.multilineHint: You can paste multiple lines at once. Duplicate entries are skipped automatically. Press Ctrl/Cmd + Enter to add quickly.
vocabulary.emptyHint: Start with a brand name, product name, or team term and future output will stay steadier.
vocabulary.addDesc: Keep only the spelling or phrasing you want to appear in final output. YAT sends these as model reference.
vocabulary.validationRequired: Please enter at least one word or phrase.
vocabulary.validationDuplicate: Those entries already exist, so there is nothing new to add.
vocabulary.listDesc: YAT sends these as preferred wording and spelling for the model, and you can edit or delete them directly here.
vocabulary.editHint: Press Enter to save or Esc to cancel.

history.retentionReduceBody: Entries older than the new limit will be permanently removed. This cannot be undone even if you increase the period later. Continue?
history.emptyHint: Start recording and recent results will appear here.
history.confirmClearOld: Clear all expired entries? This cannot be undone.
history.copyFailedBody: YAT could not write to the clipboard this time. Try again, or check whether the current environment allows clipboard access.
history.retentionWarningBody: The current setting is under 24 hours. Older entries will be deleted automatically on the next launch.

metrics.retentionHint: Entries older than this can be cleaned up regularly.

history.pageDesc: Review transcription results and manage retention.
history.controlsDesc: Set the retention window, then search or clear older entries when needed.
history.resultsDesc: Each entry should be quick to copy, retry, or delete.

capsule.dismissedHint: Recording was too short and has been dismissed.
capsule.noSpeechHint: No speech was detected in this recording.
capsule.clipboardFallbackHint: Paste failed, but the text is still in your clipboard.
capsule.clipboardFallbackHintWindows: Paste failed, but the text is still in your clipboard. On Windows this often happens when the target app is running elevated or simulated input is blocked.
capsule.clipboardFallbackHintMacos: Paste failed, but the text is still in your clipboard. Make sure YAT has Accessibility permission and the target app accepts simulated input.
capsule.errorUnknown: Something went wrong, but no detailed reason was provided.
